California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
February 27′  ■990
Dana Appling
sacramento Municipal Utility District
P.O. Box 1583 o
Sacramento, cA 958 52 -18 3 0
Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
our File No. I-90-058
Dear l{s. Appling:
You have requested advice regarding the interpretation of
Section a72oo of the Political Reforn Act (the 'Act"1 ,r 
Because
your inquiry is of a general nature and does not refer to the
reporting requirenents of specific individuals, we treat-your
request for advice as a request for infornal assistance.z This
letter confirns telephone advice provided February 22, L99o.
OUESTION
Are the filing requirenents imposed upon rrother public of-
ficials who manage public investrnents" under Section 87200 linited
to persons who nanage city, county, or state funds?
CONCLUSION
The tern rrother public officials trho Danage pubJ.ic invest-
Dentsrt is not linited to persons rrho manage the investnent of
city, county, or state funds. It includes those who rDanage any
public funds, including the investment of funds of a municipal
utility 
district.
・  Government Code Sections 8■
000-9■0■5。  A■■ statutory references
are to the Covernment Code un■
ess otherw■se ■ndicatedo  Comm■
ss■on
regulations appear at 2 Ca■
ifornia Code of Regu■
ations section
■8000′  et seo.  A■l references to regu■
ations are to Tit■
e 2′
Division 6 of the California code of Regu■
ations.
2  .nfoこwal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
imllunity provided by an opinion or foこ
wal written advice。
(Government code Section 33■
■4, 2 Calo Code of Regs.
Section 
■8329(c)(3)。
)
428 J Street,Suitc 800 ● P.o.Box 807 0 Sacramcnto CA 95804´ 0807 ● (916)322・5660


Our Fi■e No. ■-90-058
Page 2
FACTS
You are an attorney for the sacramento Municipal Utitity
District (the I'Districtrr), a public agency. District revenues
from rates charged customers and the return on investuent of the
surplus revenues are public funds, but they are not city, county,
or state funds. You have requested advice as to whether recent
amendments to Sectj.on 872oo of the Act apply to persons Lrho nanage
the District's 
investments in light of advice contained in our
L989/9O manual for Statement of Economic Interests.
ANALYSIS
Section 872oo of the Act was amended effective January I,
1990, to include additional persons rrho must file Form 72I, state-
roent of Econornic Interests, with the Conmission. Persons who are
included in Section 87200 are subject to broad conflict-of-
interest disclosure requirements. As a result of the recent
aDendment, Section 87200 now applies to city and county treasurers
and rtother pubtic officials 
who manage public investments.rl
The attached letter to interested persons dated February 27,
1990, sets forth in general tenos the types of persons who are
included within the term rrother public officials 
who nanage public
investnents. rr As indicated, the terD covers persons who nanage
the investnents of all public agencies and is not linited to those
persons who nanage city, county or state funds.
Your letter refers to a provision in our 1989/90 Statenent of
Economic Interest Eanual setting forth sooe interirn gnridelines for
interpretation of the persons covered. That nanual provides that
Section 87200 now applies to:
A■■ other public
investments′  including
officials 
who nanage public
but not linited to:
Enployees and consultants rrho have decision
naking authority with regard to investuent of city,
county or state funds. This includes, !U!-LS-10.9!
Lfu!@!_E9, pension fund managers for oublic
aqencies....
(1989/90 manual Fonn 730,
enphasis added. )
As the above excerpt indicates, the tern trpublic investnentsrr is
not linited to the investment of city, county, or state funds.
We are currently in the process of developing regulations to
implement and clarify the scope of the tera rrother public of-
ficials who manage public investnents. " Please contact us if you
have any questions with respect to the application of the tern to
specific individuals.


Our File No. r-9o-o58
Page 3
The Act does not contain a provision pernitting us to uaive
or extend filing dates for specific individuals. 
As I indicated
to you in our conversation of February 22, individuals corning
within the category of public officials 
rrho Danage public
j,nvestnents, rrho have been filing 
Form 731, rnay continue to do so
for the 1989 reporting period. If you have any first-tiue 
filers
who qualify under Section 87200, they Bust file Forrn 721 for the
1989 reporting period. The filing dates are set forth in footnote
2 of the attached letter.
I trust that this anslrers your questions reqarding this is-
sue. If you require any additional infornation, you nay contact
ne at (9I5) 322'59OL.
sincerefy,
Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel
By: I{argaret w. Ellison
counsel, Legat Division
KED : MwE: aa
Attachnent
